Recovered bricked Arduino Mega. Bootloader was intact but sketch flash was corrupted from undervoltage. Flashed blink test to verify chip alive, then flashed main_mega v1.2. All serial commands verified working.
Recovered second bricked Arduino Mega (Health). Same root cause: flash corruption from undervoltage. Flashed blink test, then health_mega v1.2. All serial commands verified.

Root cause found: Pi 3 avrdude remote flashing with -D flag corrupted bootloader when with_bootloader.hex was used. Also ModemManager was interfering on Health Pi (now disabled). 5V on barrel jack was also wrong (needs 7-12V). Fix: flash from dev PC via arduino-cli, then connect to Pi. Health Mega now operational on Pi 3.

Session ended by user. Major progress: recovered both bricked Megas, identified bootloader corruption from avrdude -D flag, disabled ModemManager on Health Pi, both Megas operational on Pis. DHT sensor wiring still needs debugging.

action_data.notes: Do NOT flash from Pi via avrdude. Always flash from dev PC with arduino-cli.

Rewrote SPEC-014 v2.0 for Level 3 privilege-separated architecture. Key changes: three-user model (human/agent/dttp), OS-enforced write restrictions, iptables network blocking, credential isolation, 5-phase safe development path (build under current perms, test, shadow, permission switch, live). tasks.title: DTTP Phase 2: Unit Testing
tasks.description: Test all gateway operations: valid/invalid spec, jurisdiction, locks, role auth, local writes, SSH deploys, FTP syncs, fail-closed behaviour. Tests run against test data, not live ADS. See SPEC-014 Phase 2.

tasks.title: DTTP Phase 3: Shadow Mode
tasks.description: Run DTTP in observation mode for 3+ agent sessions. Monitor file modifications, evaluate DTTP decisions, compare with actual outcomes. Log to shadow.log. Zero system impact. See SPEC-014 Phase 3.

tasks.title: DTTP Phase 4: Permission Switch (HUMAN ONLY)
tasks.description: Create Linux users (agent, dttp), transfer project ownership, set up iptables rules, configure credential isolation, verify all restrictions. HUMAN ONLY -- no agent may perform this. See SPEC-014 Phase 4.

tasks.title: DTTP Phase 5: Live Enforcement
tasks.description: Launch AI tools as restricted agent user. Verify all operations go through DTTP. Update AI_PROTOCOL.md and agent prompts. See SPEC-014 Phase 5.

tasks.title: Update ADT Help & Principles Page
tasks.description: Implement the 12 sections defined in SPEC-016: What is ADT, Four Pillars, ADS, Integrity Chain, SDD, DTTP, Operational Center, Roles, Narrative Workflows, Real Incidents, Glossary, and Roadmap. Update adt_panel/about.html.

tasks.title: Build ADT Operational Center Web UI
tasks.description: Implement the human web interface for the ADT Operational Center: Dashboard, ADS Timeline, Spec Registry, Task Board, Escalation Center, Agent Sessions, DTTP Monitor, and Mirror Export. See SPEC-015 Phase 2.

tasks.title: Health Pi Connectivity Investigation
tasks.description: Investigate and resolve Health Pi (192.168.43.49) reachability issues. STATUS: Pi is ONLINE and reachable. Serial ports detected. Permission issue resolved via dialout group addition.

requests.subject: ADT Panel Not Updated After Sync Request
requests.message: User reports panel at oceanpulse.pt/adt_panel/ was not updated despite requesting Overseer to sync. Investigate and ensure data.json is being uploaded correctly. Also integrate new SPEC-003 views (Task Board, Hierarchy, Delegation Map).

requests.subject: SPEC-000 (Target Architecture) Pending Definition
requests.message: SPEC-000 defines the North Star for OceanPulse. It is currently a placeholder. ALL agents should remind user that this spec needs to be defined before major architectural decisions. Check `_cortex/specs/General/SPEC-000_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE.md` at session start.

requests.subject: TIMESTAMP INTEGRITY - Use System Time
requests.message: Both CLAUDE and GEMINI have been logging events with hardcoded future timestamps instead of using `$(date -u)`. This violates ADT Constitution Article I Section 1.2 (Append-Only Integrity). Going forward, ALL timestamps MUST use system time. See updated AI_PROTOCOL.md Section 1.1.1.

requests.subject: ADT Panel Deployment Capability
requests.message: You have been granted jurisdiction to deploy the ADT Panel UI. A new script `adt_panel/deploy.sh` is available for this purpose. It compiles the latest ADS data and uploads web assets to `oceanpulse.pt/adt_panel/`. Please use this when updating the dashboard interface.

requests.subject: Update MEMORY_BANK.md - Health Pi IP Changed
requests.message: During Health Pi WiFi configuration, the IP address changed from `192.168.43.50` to `192.168.43.49` (DHCP assigned). Please update `_cortex/MEMORY_BANK.md` Section "System B (Health/Router)" with the correct IP. Also note: the WiFi connection was switched from "lab-wifi" to "WP6" to match Main Pi's network.
requests.status: COMPLETED (2026-02-01) - Memory Bank updated to .49 and WP6.

requests.subject: SPEC-012 (Integration & Enclosure) Draft Review
requests.message: I have drafted SPEC-012 (M7) in `_cortex/roles/Integration_Engineer/DRAFT_SPEC-012.md`. This includes pinout standards for Weipu SP21/SP13 connectors and a galvanic isolation strategy for the HDG frame. Please review and authorize its movement to `_cortex/specs/Hardware/`.

requests.subject: Hardware Readiness Audit (As-Procured vs. Spec)
requests.message: I have audited the lab inventory against PROCUREMENT_ORDER_PHASE_1.md. We have confirmed the presence of RPi 5, RPi 3 B+, 2x Arduino Megas, and LoRa-E5 modules. The Green Cell 50Ah battery and Arducam IMX291 are pending arrival. Please update SPEC-000 and SPEC-002 to reflect these *actual* component specs to ensure design constraints (thermal, physical, electrical) are grounded in reality.

requests.subject: SPEC-007 (LoRa Tester) Draft Review
requests.message: I have drafted SPEC-007 in _cortex/specs/Hardware/SPEC-007_LORA_TESTER.md. This defines the LoRa Test & Configuration tool (lora_tester.py) required for milestone M2.5. Please review and authorize its status to APPROVED so I can proceed with implementation.
requests.status: COMPLETED (2026-02-06) - SPEC-007 APPROVED and tool verified.

requests.subject: System C (Onshore Gateway) Ready for LoRa Setup
requests.message: System C (node1@192.168.43.136) has been integrated into the Memory Bank. Please establish the LoRa listener/bridge software on this node to facilitate end-to-end communication testing as per SPEC-008.

requests.subject: SPEC-007-UI (LoRa Test Panel) Draft Review
requests.message: I have drafted the UI/UX spec for the LoRa Test Panel in _cortex/roles/Frontend_Engineer/DRAFT_SPEC-007-UI.md. This adds a web-based diagnostic interface for Milestone M2.5. Please review and formalize it into the official specs directory so I can proceed with the dashboard integration.

specs_content.SPEC-002_COMPONENT_INTEGRATION.md:
# SPEC-002: Component Integration & Serial Protocol

**Status:** APPROVED
**Priority:** HIGH
**Owner:** Systems_Architect + Embedded_Engineer
**Created:** 2026-01-31
**References:** SPEC-000 (Target Architecture)

---

## 1. Purpose

Define the hardware connections, serial protocol, and test firmware for OceanPulse Phase 1 prototype validation.

---

## 2. Current Hardware Setup (As Connected)

### 2.1 Main Circuit

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         MAIN CIRCUIT                              │
│                                                                   │
│                      ┌─────────────────┐                          │
│       USB ┌─────────►│ Raspberry Pi    │◄─────────┐ USB           │
│           │          │ (main)          │          │               │
│           │          │                 │          │               │
│           │          │ WiFi: Connected │          │               │
│           │          └────────┬────────┘          │               │
│           │                   │ LAN               │               │
│  ┌────────┴───────┐           │          ┌───────┴────────┐       │
│  │ LoRa Board     │           │          │ Arduino Mega   │       │
│  │                │           │          │                │       │
│  └────────────────┘           │          │ Pin 2: Relay   │       │
│                               │          │ A0: TDS (3.3V) │       │
│                               │          └────────────────┘       │
└───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┘
                                │
                                │
┌───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                               │                                   │
│                      ┌────────┴────────┐                          │
│       USB ┌─────────►│ Raspberry Pi    │◄─────────┐ USB           │
│           │          │ (health)        │          │               │
│           │          │                 │          │               │
│           │          │ WiFi: Connected │          │               │
│           │          └─────────────────┘          │               │
│           │                                       │               │
│  ┌────────┴───────┐                      ┌───────┴────────┐       │
│  │ LoRa Board     │                      │ Arduino Mega   │       │
│  │                │                      │ (identical)    │       │
│  └────────────────┘                      └────────────────┘       │
│                                                                   │
│                         HEALTH CIRCUIT                            │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Connection Summary

| Circuit | Component | Connects To | Interface | Port/Pin |
|---------|-----------|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **Main** | LoRa Board | Pi (main) | USB | /dev/ttyUSB0 (verify) |
| **Main** | Arduino Mega | Pi (main) | USB | /dev/ttyACM0 (verify) |
| **Main** | Test Relay | Arduino Mega | Digital | Pin 2 |
| **Main** | Test TDS Sensor | Arduino Mega | Analog | A0 (3.3V power) |
| **Health** | LoRa Board | Pi (health) | USB | /dev/ttyUSB0 (verify) |
| **Health** | Arduino Mega | Pi (health) | USB | /dev/ttyACM0 (verify) |
| **Inter-Pi** | LAN Cable | Both Pis | Ethernet | Direct connection |
| **Network** | WiFi | Both Pis | Wireless | Home network |

> **Key:** Pi is the hub. Both LoRa and Arduino connect directly to Pi via USB (parallel, not serial).

---

## 3. Serial Protocol (Pi ↔ Arduino)

### 3.1 Physical Layer

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Baud Rate | 115200 |
| Data Bits | 8 |
| Parity | None |
| Stop Bits | 1 |
| Flow Control | None |

### 3.2 Message Format

```
<CMD>:<PARAM>\n

Examples:
  RELAY:ON\n      → Turn relay ON
  RELAY:OFF\n     → Turn relay OFF
  TDS:READ\n      → Request TDS reading
  PING\n          → Heartbeat check
```

### 3.3 Response Format

```
<CMD>:<STATUS>:<VALUE>\n

Examples:
  RELAY:OK:ON\n       → Relay turned on successfully
  RELAY:OK:OFF\n      → Relay turned off successfully
  TDS:OK:523\n        → TDS reading is 523
  PING:OK:ALIVE\n     → Arduino responsive
  ERROR:<msg>\n       → Error occurred
```

---

## 4. Test Firmware Requirements

### 4.1 Arduino Mega (Main Circuit)

**File:** `firmware/main_mega/main_mega.ino`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Init | 115200 baud, listen for commands | Must |
| Relay Control | Pin 2, respond to RELAY:ON/OFF | Must |
| TDS Read | A0, respond to TDS:READ with raw value | Must |
| Heartbeat | Respond to PING with PING:OK:ALIVE | Must |
| Error Handling | Return ERROR:<msg> on invalid command | Should |

### 4.2 Arduino Mega (Health Circuit)

**File:** `firmware/health_mega/health_mega.ino`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Init | 115200 baud, listen for commands | Must |
| Heartbeat | Respond to PING with PING:OK:ALIVE | Must |
| Restart Relay | [Future] Control main circuit power | Phase 1+ |

### 4.3 Raspberry Pi (Main)

**File:** `bridge/main_bridge.py`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Connect | Open /dev/ttyACM0 at 115200 | Must |
| Send Commands | RELAY:ON, RELAY:OFF, TDS:READ | Must |
| Parse Responses | Extract status and values | Must |
| LoRa TX | [Future] Forward to onshore | Phase 1+ |

### 4.4 Raspberry Pi (Health)

**File:** `bridge/health_bridge.py`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Connect | Open /dev/ttyACM0 at 115200 | Must |
| Heartbeat | Periodic PING to Arduino | Must |
| LAN Comms | [Future] Communicate with Main Pi | Phase 1+ |

---

## 5. Test Sequence

### 5.1 Basic Validation

```
1. Upload main_mega.ino to Main Arduino
2. Upload health_mega.ino to Health Arduino
3. Run main_bridge.py on Main Pi
4. Execute test sequence:

   a) PING → Expect PING:OK:ALIVE
   b) TDS:READ → Expect TDS:OK:<value>
   c) RELAY:ON → Expect RELAY:OK:ON (verify LED/click)
   d) RELAY:OFF → Expect RELAY:OK:OFF

5. Repeat for Health circuit (PING only for now)
```

### 5.2 Success Criteria

- [ ] Main Arduino responds to PING
- [ ] Main Arduino returns TDS readings
- [ ] Main Arduino controls relay (ON/OFF verified)
- [ ] Health Arduino responds to PING
- [ ] Both Pis can communicate with their Arduinos
- [ ] Pis can reach each other via LAN (ping test)

---

## 6. File Structure

```
oceanpulse_phase_one/
├── firmware/
│   ├── main_mega/
│   │   └── main_mega.ino
│   └── health_mega/
│       └── health_mega.ino
├── bridge/
│   ├── main_bridge.py
│   └── health_bridge.py
└── _cortex/
    └── specs/
        └── Hardware/
            └── SPEC-002_COMPONENT_INTEGRATION.md
```

---

## 7. Role Assignments

| Task | Role | Agent |
|------|------|-------|
| Spec maintenance | Systems_Architect | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Arduino firmware | Embedded_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Pi bridge scripts | Network_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Deploy tooling | Embedded_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |

---

## 8. Automated Development Pipeline

Embedded_Engineer must be able to **write, deploy, flash, and test** firmware automatically without manual intervention.

### 8.1 Target Systems (from MEMORY_BANK.md)

| System | Hostname | IP | User | Pass | Arduino Port |
|--------|----------|-----|------|------|--------------|
| **Main Pi** | main | 192.168.43.37 | lab | 777 | /dev/ttyACM0 |
| **Health Pi** | health | 192.168.43.50 | router | 777 | /dev/ttyACM0 |

### 8.2 Required Tools on Each Pi

```bash
# Install on both Pis:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y arduino-cli python3-serial

# Configure arduino-cli
arduino-cli config init
arduino-cli core update-index
arduino-cli core install arduino:avr

# Add user to dialout for serial access
sudo usermod -a -G dialout $USER
```

### 8.3 SSH Configuration (Dev Machine)

```bash
# ~/.ssh/config (add these entries)
Host main
    HostName 192.168.43.37
    User lab
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

Host health
    HostName 192.168.43.50
    User router
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

# For passwordless access (recommended):
ssh-copy-id lab@192.168.43.37
ssh-copy-id router@192.168.43.50
```

### 8.4 Deploy & Flash Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  EMBEDDED ENGINEER WORKFLOW                      │
│                                                                  │
│  1. WRITE      firmware/main_mega/main_mega.ino                 │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  2. DEPLOY     scp firmware/ → Pi:/tmp/firmware/                │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  3. FLASH      ssh Pi "arduino-cli compile && upload"           │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  4. TEST       ssh Pi "python3 test_serial.py"                  │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  5. VERIFY     Check output: PING:OK:ALIVE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 8.5 Deployment Scripts

**File:** `firmware/deploy.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Deploy and flash firmware to OceanPulse Pis

set -e

MAIN_PI="lab@192.168.43.37"
HEALTH_PI="router@192.168.43.50"
REMOTE_DIR="/tmp/oceanpulse_firmware"
BOARD="arduino:avr:mega"
PORT="/dev/ttyACM0"

deploy_main() {
    echo "=== Deploying to MAIN Pi ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/main_mega $MAIN_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $MAIN_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/main_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Main firmware flashed ==="
}

deploy_health() {
    echo "=== Deploying to HEALTH Pi ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/health_mega $HEALTH_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/health_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Health firmware flashed ==="
}

test_main() {
    echo "=== Testing MAIN Arduino ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

test_health() {
    echo "=== Testing HEALTH Arduino ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

case "$1" in
    main)   deploy_main ;;
    health) deploy_health ;;
    all)    deploy_main && deploy_health ;;
    test)   test_main && test_health ;;
    *)      echo "Usage: $0 {main|health|all|test}" ;;
esac
```

### 8.6 Quick Test Script

**File:** `firmware/test_serial.py`

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Quick serial test for Arduino firmware"""

import serial
import sys
import time

PORT = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '/dev/ttyACM0'
BAUD = 115200

def test():
    ser = serial.Serial(PORT, BAUD, timeout=2)
    time.sleep(2)  # Wait for Arduino reset

    # Clear buffer
    ser.reset_input_buffer()

    # Test PING
    ser.write(b'PING\n')
    response = ser.readline().decode().strip()
    print(f"PING -> {response}")
    assert response == "PING:OK:ALIVE", f"PING failed: {response}"

    print("All tests passed!")
    ser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test()
```

### 8.7 Embedded_Engineer Jurisdiction Update

To support this workflow, Embedded_Engineer jurisdiction extends to:

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `firmware/` | Arduino sketches |
| `firmware/deploy.sh` | Deployment script |
| `firmware/test_serial.py` | Test utilities |

---

*This spec defines the foundation for all hardware integration in OceanPulse Phase 1.*

```bash
arduino-cli config init
arduino-cli core update-index
arduino-cli core install arduino:avr

# Add user to dialout for serial access
sudo usermod -a -G dialout $USER
```

### 8.3 SSH Configuration (Dev Machine)

```bash
# ~/.ssh/config (add these entries)
Host main
    HostName 192.168.43.37
    User lab
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

Host health
    HostName 192.168.43.50
    User router
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

# For passwordless access (recommended):
ssh-copy-id lab@192.168.43.37
ssh-copy-id router@192.168.43.50
```

### 8.4 Deploy & Flash Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  EMBEDDED ENGINEER WORKFLOW                      │
│                                                                  │
│  1. WRITE      firmware/main_mega/main_mega.ino                 │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  2. DEPLOY     scp firmware/ → Pi:/tmp/firmware/                │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  3. FLASH      ssh Pi "arduino-cli compile && upload"           │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  4. TEST       ssh Pi "python3 test_serial.py"                  │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  5. VERIFY     Check output: PING:OK:ALIVE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 8.5 Deployment Scripts

**File:** `firmware/deploy.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Deploy and flash firmware to OceanPulse Pis

set -e

MAIN_PI="lab@192.168.43.37"
HEALTH_PI="router@192.168.43.50"
REMOTE_DIR="/tmp/oceanpulse_firmware"
BOARD="arduino:avr:mega"
PORT="/dev/ttyACM0"

deploy_main() {
    echo "=== Deploying to MAIN Pi ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/main_mega $MAIN_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $MAIN_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/main_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Main firmware flashed ==="
}

deploy_health() {
    echo "=== Deploying to HEALTH Pi ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/health_mega $HEALTH_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/health_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Health firmware flashed ==="
}

test_main() {
    echo "=== Testing MAIN Arduino ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

test_health() {
    echo "=== Testing HEALTH Arduino ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

case "$1" in
    main)   deploy_main ;;
    health) deploy_health ;;
    all)    deploy_main && deploy_health ;;
    test)   test_main && test_health ;;
    *)      echo "Usage: $0 {main|health|all|test}" ;;
esac
```

### 8.6 Quick Test Script

**File:** `firmware/test_serial.py`

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Quick serial test for Arduino firmware"""

import serial
import sys
import time

PORT = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '/dev/ttyACM0'
BAUD = 115200

def test():
    ser = serial.Serial(PORT, BAUD, timeout=2)
    time.sleep(2)  # Wait for Arduino reset

    # Clear buffer
    ser.reset_input_buffer()

    # Test PING
    ser.write(b'PING\n')
    response = ser.readline().decode().strip()
    print(f"PING -> {response}")
    assert response == "PING:OK:ALIVE", f"PING failed: {response}"

    print("All tests passed!")
    ser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    test()
```

### 8.7 Embedded_Engineer Jurisdiction Update

To support this workflow, Embedded_Engineer jurisdiction extends to:

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `firmware/` | Arduino sketches |
| `firmware/deploy.sh` | Deployment script |
| `firmware/test_serial.py` | Test utilities |

---

*This spec defines the foundation for all hardware integration in OceanPulse Phase 1.*
specs_content.SPEC-002_COMPONENT_INTEGRATION:
# SPEC-002: Component Integration & Serial Protocol

**Status:** APPROVED
**Priority:** HIGH
**Owner:** Systems_Architect + Embedded_Engineer
**Created:** 2026-01-31
**References:** SPEC-000 (Target Architecture)

---

## 1. Purpose

Define the hardware connections, serial protocol, and test firmware for OceanPulse Phase 1 prototype validation.

---

## 2. Current Hardware Setup (As Connected)

### 2.1 Main Circuit

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         MAIN CIRCUIT                              │
│                                                                   │
│                      ┌─────────────────┐                          │
│       USB ┌─────────►│ Raspberry Pi    │◄─────────┐ USB           │
│           │          │ (main)          │          │               │
│           │          │                 │          │               │
│           │          │ WiFi: Connected │          │               │
│           │          └────────┬────────┘          │               │
│           │                   │ LAN               │               │
│  ┌────────┴───────┐           │          ┌───────┴────────┐       │
│  │ LoRa Board     │           │          │ Arduino Mega   │       │
│  │                │           │          │                │       │
│  └────────────────┘           │          │ Pin 2: Relay   │       │
│                               │          │ A0: TDS (3.3V) │       │
│                               │          └────────────────┘       │
└───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┘
                                │
                                │
┌───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┐
│                               │                                   │
│                      ┌────────┴────────┐                          │
│       USB ┌─────────►│ Raspberry Pi    │◄─────────┐ USB           │
│           │          │ (health)        │          │               │
│           │          │                 │          │               │
│           │          │ WiFi: Connected │          │               │
│           │          └─────────────────┘          │               │
│           │                                       │               │
│  ┌────────┴───────┐                      ┌───────┴────────┐       │
│  │ LoRa Board     │                      │ Arduino Mega   │       │
│  │                │                      │ (identical)    │       │
│  └────────────────┘                      └────────────────┘       │
│                                                                   │
│                         HEALTH CIRCUIT                            │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2.2 Connection Summary

| Circuit | Component | Connects To | Interface | Port/Pin |
|---------|-----------|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **Main** | LoRa Board | Pi (main) | USB | /dev/ttyUSB0 (verify) |
| **Main** | Arduino Mega | Pi (main) | USB | /dev/ttyACM0 (verify) |
| **Main** | Test Relay | Arduino Mega | Digital | Pin 2 |
| **Main** | Test TDS Sensor | Arduino Mega | Analog | A0 (3.3V power) |
| **Health** | LoRa Board | Pi (health) | USB | /dev/ttyUSB0 (verify) |
| **Health** | Arduino Mega | Pi (health) | USB | /dev/ttyACM0 (verify) |
| **Inter-Pi** | LAN Cable | Both Pis | Ethernet | Direct connection |
| **Network** | WiFi | Both Pis | Wireless | Home network |

> **Key:** Pi is the hub. Both LoRa and Arduino connect directly to Pi via USB (parallel, not serial).

---

## 3. Serial Protocol (Pi ↔ Arduino)

### 3.1 Physical Layer

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Baud Rate | 115200 |
| Data Bits | 8 |
| Parity | None |
| Stop Bits | 1 |
| Flow Control | None |

### 3.2 Message Format

```
<CMD>:<PARAM>\n

Examples:
  RELAY:ON\n      → Turn relay ON
  RELAY:OFF\n     → Turn relay OFF
  TDS:READ\n      → Request TDS reading
  PING\n          → Heartbeat check
```

### 3.3 Response Format

```
<CMD>:<STATUS>:<VALUE>\n

Examples:
  RELAY:OK:ON\n       → Relay turned on successfully
  RELAY:OK:OFF\n      → Relay turned off successfully
  TDS:OK:523\n        → TDS reading is 523
  PING:OK:ALIVE\n     → Arduino responsive
  ERROR:<msg>\n       → Error occurred
```

---

## 4. Test Firmware Requirements

### 4.1 Arduino Mega (Main Circuit)

**File:** `firmware/main_mega/main_mega.ino`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Init | 115200 baud, listen for commands | Must |
| Relay Control | Pin 2, respond to RELAY:ON/OFF | Must |
| TDS Read | A0, respond to TDS:READ with raw value | Must |
| Heartbeat | Respond to PING with PING:OK:ALIVE | Must |
| Error Handling | Return ERROR:<msg> on invalid command | Should |

### 4.2 Arduino Mega (Health Circuit)

**File:** `firmware/health_mega/health_mega.ino`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Init | 115200 baud, listen for commands | Must |
| Heartbeat | Respond to PING with PING:OK:ALIVE | Must |
| Restart Relay | [Future] Control main circuit power | Phase 1+ |

### 4.3 Raspberry Pi (Main)

**File:** `bridge/main_bridge.py`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Connect | Open /dev/ttyACM0 at 115200 | Must |
| Send Commands | RELAY:ON, RELAY:OFF, TDS:READ | Must |
| Parse Responses | Extract status and values | Must |
| LoRa TX | [Future] Forward to onshore | Phase 1+ |

### 4.4 Raspberry Pi (Health)

**File:** `bridge/health_bridge.py`

| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| Serial Connect | Open /dev/ttyACM0 at 115200 | Must |
| Heartbeat | Periodic PING to Arduino | Must |
| LAN Comms | [Future] Communicate with Main Pi | Phase 1+ |

---

## 5. Test Sequence

### 5.1 Basic Validation

```
1. Upload main_mega.ino to Main Arduino
2. Upload health_mega.ino to Health Arduino
3. Run main_bridge.py on Main Pi
4. Execute test sequence:

   a) PING → Expect PING:OK:ALIVE
   b) TDS:READ → Expect TDS:OK:<value>
   c) RELAY:ON → Expect RELAY:OK:ON (verify LED/click)
   d) RELAY:OFF → Expect RELAY:OK:OFF

5. Repeat for Health circuit (PING only for now)
```

### 5.2 Success Criteria

- [ ] Main Arduino responds to PING
- [ ] Main Arduino returns TDS readings
- [ ] Main Arduino controls relay (ON/OFF verified)
- [ ] Health Arduino responds to PING
- [ ] Both Pis can communicate with their Arduinos
- [ ] Pis can reach each other via LAN (ping test)

---

## 6. File Structure

```
oceanpulse_phase_one/
├── firmware/
│   ├── main_mega/
│   │   └── main_mega.ino
│   └── health_mega/
│       └── health_mega.ino
├── bridge/
│   ├── main_bridge.py
│   └── health_bridge.py
└── _cortex/
    └── specs/
        └── Hardware/
            └── SPEC-002_COMPONENT_INTEGRATION.md
```

---

## 7. Role Assignments

| Task | Role | Agent |
|------|------|-------|
| Spec maintenance | Systems_Architect | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Arduino firmware | Embedded_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Pi bridge scripts | Network_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |
| Deploy tooling | Embedded_Engineer | CLAUDE/GEMINI |

---

## 8. Automated Development Pipeline

Embedded_Engineer must be able to **write, deploy, flash, and test** firmware automatically without manual intervention.

### 8.1 Target Systems (from MEMORY_BANK.md)

| System | Hostname | IP | User | Pass | Arduino Port |
|--------|----------|-----|------|------|--------------|
| **Main Pi** | main | 192.168.43.37 | lab | 777 | /dev/ttyACM0 |
| **Health Pi** | health | 192.168.43.50 | router | 777 | /dev/ttyACM0 |

### 8.2 Required Tools on Each Pi

```bash
# Install on both Pis:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y arduino-cli python3-serial

# Configure arduino-cli
arduino-cli config init
arduino-cli core update-index
arduino-cli core install arduino:avr

# Add user to dialout for serial access
sudo usermod -a -G dialout $USER
```

### 8.3 SSH Configuration (Dev Machine)

```bash
# ~/.ssh/config (add these entries)
Host main
    HostName 192.168.43.37
    User lab
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

Host health
    HostName 192.168.43.50
    User router
    StrictHostKeyChecking no

# For passwordless access (recommended):
ssh-copy-id lab@192.168.43.37
ssh-copy-id router@192.168.43.50
```

### 8.4 Deploy & Flash Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  EMBEDDED ENGINEER WORKFLOW                      │
│                                                                  │
│  1. WRITE      firmware/main_mega/main_mega.ino                 │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  2. DEPLOY     scp firmware/ → Pi:/tmp/firmware/                │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  3. FLASH      ssh Pi "arduino-cli compile && upload"           │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  4. TEST       ssh Pi "python3 test_serial.py"                  │
│       │                                                          │
│       ▼                                                          │
│  5. VERIFY     Check output: PING:OK:ALIVE                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 8.5 Deployment Scripts

**File:** `firmware/deploy.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Deploy and flash firmware to OceanPulse Pis

set -e

MAIN_PI="lab@192.168.43.37"
HEALTH_PI="router@192.168.43.50"
REMOTE_DIR="/tmp/oceanpulse_firmware"
BOARD="arduino:avr:mega"
PORT="/dev/ttyACM0"

deploy_main() {
    echo "=== Deploying to MAIN Pi ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/main_mega $MAIN_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $MAIN_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/main_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Main firmware flashed ==="
}

deploy_health() {
    echo "=== Deploying to HEALTH Pi ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "mkdir -p $REMOTE_DIR"
    scp -r firmware/health_mega $HEALTH_PI:$REMOTE_DIR/
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "cd $REMOTE_DIR/health_mega && arduino-cli compile -b $BOARD && arduino-cli upload -b $BOARD -p $PORT"
    echo "=== Health firmware flashed ==="
}

test_main() {
    echo "=== Testing MAIN Arduino ==="
    ssh $MAIN_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

test_health() {
    echo "=== Testing HEALTH Arduino ==="
    ssh $HEALTH_PI "echo 'PING' | timeout 2 cat > $PORT; timeout 2 cat < $PORT"
}

case "$1" in
    main)   deploy_main ;;
    health) deploy_health ;;
    all)    deploy_main && deploy_health ;;
    test)   test_main && test_health ;;
    *)      echo "Usage: $0 {main|health|all|test}" ;;
esac
```

### 8.6 Quick Test Script

**File:** `firmware/test_serial.py`

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Quick serial test for Arduino firmware"""

import serial
import sys
import time

PORT = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '/dev/ttyACM0'
BAUD = 115200

def test():
    ser = serial.Serial(PORT, BAUD, timeout=2)
    time.sleep(2)  # Wait for Arduino reset

    # Clear buffer
    ser.reset_input_buffer()

    # Test PING
    ser.write(b'PING\n')
    response = ser.readline().decode().strip()
    print(f"PING -> {response}")
    assert response == "PING:OK:ALIVE", f"PING failed: {response}"

    print("All tests passed!")
    ser.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":

```

specs_content.SPEC-007_LORA_TESTER.md:
# SPEC-007: LoRa Test & Configuration Tool

**Status:** DRAFT
**Priority:** HIGH
**Owner:** Network_Engineer
**Created:** 2026-02-06
**References:** SPEC-000, SPEC-004 (Milestone M2.5)

---

## 1. Purpose

Define the requirements and implementation for a LoRa testing and configuration tool to optimize the radio link between the buoy and onshore gateway using Meshtastic firmware on Seeed LoRa-E5 modules.

---

## 2. Hardware Environment

- **Modules:** Seeed LoRa-E5 Mini (STM32WLE5)
- **Firmware:** Meshtastic (Serial Interface enabled)
- **Interface:** USB Serial (/dev/ttyUSB0 typically)
- **Host:** Raspberry Pi (Main/Health) and Onshore Gateway

---

## 3. Functional Requirements

The tool (`comms/lora_tester.py`) shall provide:

### 3.1 Parameter Configuration
- **Region Set:** Ability to set LoRa region (e.g., EU_868).
- **Modem Presets:** Switch between Meshtastic presets:
    - `SHORT_FAST`
    - `LONG_SLOW`
    - `VERY_LONG_SLOW`
- **Channel Config:** Set frequency and bandwidth if not using presets.

### 3.2 Live Metrics
- **RSSI Monitoring:** Signal strength of received packets.
- **SNR Monitoring:** Signal-to-noise ratio.
- **Distance Estimation:** (If GPS available on modules).

### 3.3 Test Modes
- **Ping-Pong:** Send a packet and wait for acknowledgment to measure Round Trip Time (RTT).
- **Stress Test:** Send N packets at specific intervals to measure Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR).
- **Continuous Receive:** Listen and log all incoming traffic with metadata.

### 3.4 Data Logging
- Append test results to `comms/logs/lora_tests.csv`.
- Fields: `timestamp`, `preset`, `rssi`, `snr`, `pdr`, `latency`.

---

## 4. Implementation Strategy

### 4.1 Python Dependencies
- `meshtastic` (Python API)
- `pubsub` (for event handling)
- `pandas` (for log analysis - optional)

### 4.2 CLI Interface
```bash
python3 comms/lora_tester.py --mode ping --count 10 --preset LONG_SLOW
python3 comms/lora_tester.py --mode monitor
python3 comms/lora_tester.py --set-preset SHORT_FAST
```

---

## 5. Success Criteria

- [ ] Tool can successfully connect to LoRa-E5 via Meshtastic API.
- [ ] Tool can change modem presets and verify the change.
- [ ] Tool can measure RSSI and SNR for received packets.
- [ ] Ping-Pong test works between two modules at <10m range.
- [ ] Results are logged to a CSV file.

---

## 6. Jurisdiction

This tool and its logs reside within the `comms/` directory, under the jurisdiction of the **Network_Engineer**.

- Dashboard updates for real-time visualization.

---
*Note: This is a placeholder. Detailed definition is pending.*
specs_content.SPEC-012_INTEGRATION.md:
# SPEC-012: Physical Integration & Enclosure

**Status:** DRAFT
**Priority:** HIGH
**Owner:** Integration_Engineer (build), Systems_Architect (spec)
**Created:** 2026-02-05
**References:** SPEC-000 (Target Architecture), SPEC-005 (Dev Sensor Platform), SPEC-006 (Health Circuit), SPEC-009 (Oil Detection), SPEC-010 (Power Autonomy)
**Source Documents:** Final Drawings of customed buoy.pdf, Door Panel Technical Requirements (DEXON Marine), Mauser.pt invoices 2025EC1351975 & 2026EC1375416, HARDWARE_AUDIT.md

---

## 1. Purpose

This specification defines the physical integration of all OceanPulse electronic subsystems into the Model 2600Fx buoy frame. It covers zone layout, shelf assignments, cable routing, hull penetrations, weight distribution, thermal management, and assembly sequence -- all with millimetric precision derived from the manufacturer's technical drawings.

---

## 2. Buoy Frame Dimensions (Model 2600Fx)

Source: `Final Drawings of customed buoy.pdf`

### 2.1 Overall Dimensions

| Measurement | Value | Notes |
|-------------|-------|-------|
| **Float diameter** | 1200mm | Circular HDPE float body |
| **Float height** | 600mm | Vertical height of float body |
| **Keel depth** | 130mm | Below float bottom |
| **Tower frame height** | 782mm | Structural frame from float top |
| **Total above float** | 1025mm | Tower + top platform + light mount |
| **Total structure height** | 1755mm | Keel bottom to top (130 + 600 + 1025) |
| **Frame weight** | 80kg | Steel frame only, excluding electronics |

### 2.2 Tower Structure

| Measurement | Value | Source |
|-------------|-------|--------|
| **Top platform** | 461.2 x 461.2mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Internal tower height** | 974mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening height** | 824.5mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening width (bottom)** | 419.8mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening width (top)** | 333.6mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements (trapezoidal) |
| **Door material** | Carbon steel Q235 | Current frame: raw steel (dev unit). HDG-identical frame arriving in ~20-25 days (ETA late Feb 2026). Final integration uses HDG frame only. |

### 2.3 Tower Interior Layout

The tower interior contains **2 shelves** with **2 watertight boxes** (one per shelf).

| Element | Position | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| **Lower shelf** | Lower tower section | Holds the larger watertight box |
| **Upper shelf** | Upper tower section | Holds the smaller watertight box |
| **Lower watertight box** | On lower shelf | Slightly larger than upper box |
| **Upper watertight box** | On upper shelf | Slightly smaller than lower box |

Drawing reference dimensions (side view, 塑料防水盒 annotation):

| Measurement | Value | Interpretation |
|-------------|-------|----------------|
| Box width | 300mm | Width of watertight boxes |
| Interval 1 | 170mm | Lower box or spacing |
| Interval 2 | 197mm | Shelf gap or larger box height |
| Interval 3 | 170mm | Upper box or spacing |

> **NOTE:** Exact internal dimensions of each watertight box to be measured from physical unit. The drawing intervals (170/197/170mm) require physical verification against the 2-shelf layout.

---

## 3. Zone Layout

The buoy is divided into four physical zones:

```
        ┌─── Zone A: Tower Top (exposed) ───┐
        │  Solar panel mount, LoRa antennas  │
        │  Nav light position, camera        │
        ├─── Zone B: Tower Interior ─────────┤  ← Door panel access
        │  All primary electronics           │
        │  Waterproof box + shelf system     │
        │  974mm internal height             │
        ├─── Zone C: Float Hull ─────────────┤  ← Sealed, no field access
        │  Battery, inverter, ballast        │
        │  Access via tower base only        │
        ├─── Zone D: Underwater ─────────────┤
        │  Sensors, UV lamp, keel            │
        │  130mm below float                 │
        └────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.1 Zone A: Tower Top (Exposed)

**Environment:** Fully exposed to weather, salt spray, UV radiation, bird strike.
**Access:** Open (no enclosure).
**IP Rating Required:** All components IP67 minimum.

| Component | Mount Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Solar panel | Top platform (461.2 x 461.2mm) | Angled bracket, SS316 bolts |
| LoRa antenna (Main) | Tower leg, upper | SMA cable to Zone B |
| LoRa antenna (Health) | Tower leg, opposite | SMA cable to Zone B |
| IMX378 Camera | Tower leg, mid-height | IP68 housing required, facing outward |
| HC-SR501 PIR sensor | **NOT HERE** -- humidity fatal | See Zone B |

### 3.2 Zone B: Tower Interior (Protected)

**Environment:** Sheltered by tower frame + door panel, not hermetically sealed.
**Access:** Door panel (trapezoidal, 419.8mm bottom x 333.6mm top x 824.5mm height).
**Protection:** Two watertight boxes on two shelves, desiccant packs inside each box.

**CRITICAL CONSTRAINT:** All components inserted through door opening must fit within 333.6mm (minimum width at top). Diagonal insertion possible for items up to ~540mm.

**Layout: 2 shelves, 2 watertight boxes**

| Location | Box | Component Assignments | Notes |
|----------|-----|----------------------|-------|
| **Lower shelf** | Larger watertight box | RPi 5 (Main) + Arduino Mega (Main) | Main circuit. Larger box accommodates RPi 5 (85x56mm) + Mega (101x53mm) + wiring. Victron MPPT 75/15 (100x113x40mm) also fits here if box allows. |
| **Lower shelf** | (outside box) | Victron MPPT 75/15 | If MPPT doesn't fit inside box, mount on shelf beside it. MPPT is not humidity-critical. |
| **Upper shelf** | Smaller watertight box | RPi 3 B+ (Health) + Arduino Mega (Health) | Health circuit. RPi 3 (85x56mm) + Mega (101x53mm). Smaller box sufficient. |
| **Upper shelf** | (outside box or in box) | LoRa Module (Main) + LoRa Module (Health) | Near top for shortest SMA antenna cable runs to Zone A. |
| **Inside either box** | With desiccant | HC-SR501 PIR | Must be sealed with desiccant (HARDWARE_AUDIT: humidity-fatal). |
| **Tower top area** | -- | Antenna SMA feedthroughs | Route through tower structure to Zone A. |
| **Tower base area** | -- | Cable management | P1 grommet entry point, drip loops, cable routing. |

> **PENDING:** Exact internal dimensions of each watertight box must be measured from the physical unit to confirm component fit. Assignment above is based on component dimensions vs. relative box sizes.

**PIR Humidity Mitigation (HARDWARE_AUDIT):** The HC-SR501 is extremely humidity-sensitive. It MUST be mounted inside one of the watertight boxes with desiccant packs. Detection window points toward door panel (which has ventilation gaps). If detection through the box wall is insufficient, mount inside a dedicated IP67 mini-enclosure with IR-transparent window.

### 3.3 Zone C: Float Hull (Sealed)

**Environment:** Sealed HDPE hull, potential condensation, no direct water ingress.
**Access:** Through tower base opening only (pre-assembly or major maintenance).
**IP Rating:** Hull itself provides IP68 when sealed.

| Component | Weight | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| LiFePO4 50Ah battery | ~6.0kg | Centre-bottom for ballast stability |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~1.5kg | Opposite side from battery for weight distribution |
| 30A Relay board | ~0.2kg | Near inverter, flyback diode REQUIRED (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| Cable bus (to Zone B) | -- | Through tower base grommet |
| Cable bus (to Zone D) | -- | Through hull bottom penetrations |

**INVERTER EMI WARNING (HARDWARE_AUDIT):** Modified sine wave inverter produces high EMI. Mount minimum 300mm from LoRa antennas. The float hull provides natural shielding from Zone B electronics. Ensure thermal venting pathway exists -- passive convection through tower base opening.

### 3.4 Zone D: Underwater (Submerged)

**Environment:** Full saltwater immersion (Sagres, Portugal -- high salinity Atlantic).
**Access:** Haul out only.
**IP Rating:** IP68 mandatory for all components and connectors.
**Material:** SS316 only (HARDWARE_AUDIT: SS304 will tea-stain in Sagres salinity).

| Component | Mount Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| DS18B20 (temperature) | Keel bracket, 130mm depth | MUST use stainless steel probe housing (HARDWARE_AUDIT: TO-92 not water-safe) |
| pH Sensor | Keel bracket | Dev only, expect drift >24h (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| TDS Sensor | Keel bracket | Requires baseline calibration (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| JSN-SR04T transducer | Hull bottom, external | IP67 rated transducer, control board in Zone C (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | Keel bracket, angled down | 230V AC via inverter, Weipu SP21 connector |

---

## 4. Hull Penetrations

All hull penetrations use Weipu SP-series IP68 connectors (HARDWARE_AUDIT). Standardize pinouts to prevent cross-wiring.

### 4.1 Penetration Map

| ID | Location | Connector | Purpose | Pin Count | Cable |
|----|----------|-----------|---------|-----------|-------|
| P1 | Float top → Tower base | Grommet (sealed) | Main cable bus (Zone C ↔ Zone B) | Multi-conductor | Tinned copper, shielded |
| P2 | Float bottom (port) | Weipu SP21 | UV lamp power (230V AC) | 3 (L/N/E) | Marine-grade, tinned |
| P3 | Float bottom (starboard) | Weipu SP13 | Sensor bundle (DS18B20, pH, TDS) | 6 | Tinned copper, shielded |
| P4 | Float bottom (fore) | Weipu SP13 | JSN-SR04T ultrasonic | 4 (VCC/Trig/Echo/GND) | Tinned copper |
| P5 | Tower top (platform edge) | SMA bulkhead x2 | LoRa antenna feedthrough | Coax | RG-174 or RG-316 |
| P6 | Tower side (upper) | Weipu SP13 | Camera cable (IMX378) | 4 | Shielded, short run |

### 4.2 Pinout Standard

**SP21 (Power/UV) -- Penetration P2:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | Live (230V AC) | Brown |
| 2 | Neutral | Blue |
| 3 | Earth | Green/Yellow |

**SP13 (Sensor Bundle) -- Penetration P3:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | DS18B20 Data (OneWire) | Yellow |
| 2 | DS18B20 VCC (3.3V) | Red |
| 3 | pH Analog Signal | White |
| 4 | TDS Analog Signal | Orange |
| 5 | Common VCC (5V) | Red/White |
| 6 | Common GND | Black |

**SP13 (Ultrasonic) -- Penetration P4:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | VCC (5V) | Red |
| 2 | Trigger | Yellow |
| 3 | Echo | Green |
| 4 | GND | Black |

---

## 5. Cable Routing

### 5.1 Zone D → Zone C (through hull bottom penetrations P2, P3, P4)

```
Sensors (keel) ──[P3]──→ Float hull interior ──→ JSN-SR04T control board
UV lamp (keel)  ──[P2]──→ Float hull interior ──→ Relay → Inverter
Ultrasonic      ──[P4]──→ Float hull interior ──→ JSN-SR04T control board
```

### 5.2 Zone C → Zone B (through tower base grommet P1)

```
Battery ──→ Victron MPPT ──[P1]──→ RPi 5 power, RPi 3 power
Sensor signals ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) analog/digital inputs
Relay control ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) digital output
JSN-SR04T signals ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) digital I/O
```

### 5.3 Zone B → Zone A (through antenna feedthrough P5, camera cable P6)

```
LoRa (Main) ──[SMA]──→ Antenna (tower leg)
LoRa (Health) ──[SMA]──→ Antenna (tower leg, opposite)
Camera ──[P6]──→ IMX378 (tower leg, mid-height)
```

### 5.4 Cable Management Rules

1. **All cables tinned copper** (HARDWARE_AUDIT: standard copper wicks salt air)
2. **Signal cables shielded** where running parallel to power cables (>100mm separation where possible)
3. **Cable ties: SS316** (nylon degrades in UV within months)
4. **Drip loops** on all cables entering sealed zones (prevent water wicking)
5. **Strain relief** at all connector interfaces
6. **Label all cables** at both ends with penetration ID (P1-P6)

---

## 6. Weight Budget

### 6.1 Component Weights

| Component | Weight (g) | Zone |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| RPi 5 4GB + case + cooler | ~120 | B |
| RPi 3 B+ + case | ~80 | B |
| Arduino Mega x2 | ~74 (37 each) | B |
| Victron MPPT 75/15 | ~200 | B |
| LoRa modules x2 | ~40 | B |
| 3.5" Touchscreen | ~80 | B |
| HC-SR501 PIR | ~15 | B |
| LiFePO4 50Ah battery | ~6000 | C |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~1500 | C |
| 30A Relay board | ~200 | C |
| JSN-SR04T control board | ~50 | C |
| Cables, connectors, misc | ~800 | All |
| DS18B20 probe (SS housing) | ~100 | D |
| pH sensor + probe | ~150 | D |
| TDS sensor + probe | ~100 | D |
| JSN-SR04T transducer | ~80 | D |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | ~500 | D |
| IMX378 camera + housing | ~100 | A |
| Solar panel | ~2000 | A |
| **TOTAL ELECTRONICS** | **~12,189** | -- |

### 6.2 Weight Distribution

| Zone | Total Weight | % of Electronics |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| A (Tower Top) | ~2,100g | 17% |
| B (Tower Interior) | ~609g | 5% |
| C (Float Hull) | ~8,550g | 70% |
| D (Underwater) | ~930g | 8% |

**Centre of gravity analysis:** 70% of electronics weight is in the float hull (Zone C), with the 6kg battery at centre-bottom. This is favourable for stability -- low centre of gravity. The 2kg solar panel on top is the main top-heavy element; the wide 1200mm float base provides adequate righting moment.

### 6.3 Total Buoy Weight

| Item | Weight |
|------|--------|
| Steel frame (Model 2600Fx) | 80.0 kg |
| Electronics (all zones) | ~12.2 kg |
| Fasteners, brackets, sealant | ~2.0 kg (est.) |
| **Total deployed weight** | **~94.2 kg** |

---

## 7. Thermal Management

### 7.1 Heat Sources

| Component | Max Power Dissipation | Zone | Mitigation |
|-----------|-----------------------|------|------------|
| RPi 5 | ~6W (under load) | B | Active cooler (procured), ventilation |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~15W (at load) | C | Passive convection through tower base |
| Victron MPPT 75/15 | ~2W | B | Passive, low concern |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | Heat at lamp (Zone D) | D | Water-cooled by ocean |

### 7.2 Summer Thermal Risk (Sagres, Portugal)

Ambient air temperature can reach 40°C in summer. Inside the sealed float hull, temperatures could reach 55-60°C without ventilation.

**Mitigations:**
1. **Float hull:** Passive convection path through tower base grommet (P1). Do NOT hermetically seal tower base.
2. **Tower interior:** Door panel gaps provide natural ventilation. Waterproof box has IP67 vents with desiccant.
3. **IMX378 camera:** Verify thermal limits for continuous summer operation (HARDWARE_AUDIT pending audit).
4. **White/light colour on float exterior** to reduce solar heat absorption.

---

## 8. Environmental Protection

### 8.1 Material Standards (HARDWARE_AUDIT)

| Category | Standard | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| External fasteners | SS316 | SS304 tea-stains in Sagres salinity |
| External brackets | SS316 or POM-C | Corrosion resistance |
| Wiring | Tinned copper | Prevents internal salt-air corrosion wicking |
| Connectors | Weipu SP21/SP13 (IP68) | Proven marine-grade |
| Hull penetration sealant | Marine-grade polyurethane | UV-resistant, flexible |
| Enclosure | IP68 (hull), IP67 (tower box) | Full submersion rated |

### 8.2 Corrosion Prevention

1. All dissimilar metal junctions insulated with nylon washers (prevent galvanic corrosion)
2. Sacrificial zinc anode on keel bracket (protect SS316 fasteners)
3. Anti-seize compound on all threaded SS316 connections (prevent galling)
4. Annual inspection cycle for connector O-ring integrity

---

## 9. Assembly Sequence

**Pre-condition:** All components tested on bench per SPEC-005, SPEC-006, SPEC-007, SPEC-008.

| Step | Action | Zone | Checkpoint |
|------|--------|------|------------|
| 1 | Install hull penetration connectors P2, P3, P4 | C/D boundary | Pressure test each penetration to 1 bar |
| 2 | Route sensor cables through P3, P4; attach to keel bracket | D | Continuity test all sensor lines |
| 3 | Mount UV lamp on keel bracket; route power through P2 | D | Insulation resistance test (230V cable) |
| 4 | Mount DS18B20, pH, TDS probes on keel bracket | D | Verify probe housing seal integrity |
| 5 | Install JSN-SR04T transducer on hull bottom; control board inside hull | C/D | Ultrasonic echo test in air |
| 6 | Install LiFePO4 battery at hull centre-bottom | C | Secure with SS316 strap, verify polarity |
| 7 | Install inverter + relay board in hull | C | Verify relay flyback diode present |
| 8 | Route main cable bus through tower base grommet P1 | C→B | Label all conductors, drip loop |
| 9 | Mount Victron MPPT on lower shelf (or inside lower box if space allows) | B | Connect battery + solar input |
| 10 | Install RPi 5 + Arduino Mega (Main) into lower watertight box; seal | B | Serial connection test |
| 11 | Install RPi 3 B+ + Arduino Mega (Health) into upper watertight box; seal | B | Serial connection test |
| 12 | Mount LoRa modules on upper shelf; route SMA to P5 | B→A | RF link test (pair) |
| 13 | Mount PIR sensor inside waterproof box with desiccant | B | Motion detection test |
| 14 | Mount IMX378 camera on tower leg; route cable through P6 | A→B | Image capture test |
| 15 | Mount solar panel on top platform; connect to MPPT | A→B | Charge voltage test |
| 16 | Install door panel; seal edges | B | Visual inspection |
| 17 | **Full system integration test** | All | All sensors reporting, relay cycling, LoRa link, health heartbeat |

---

## 10. Pending Audits (from HARDWARE_AUDIT.md)

These must be completed before final integration:

- [ ] **Everbeam 50W UV:** Confirm 230V AC current draw for inverter sizing
- [ ] **IMX378 Camera:** Verify thermal limits for summer port operation (Sagres ~40°C ambient)
- [ ] **NE555 Circuit:** Audit relay spark suppression (flyback diode) for 30A DC load
- [ ] **Float buoyancy:** Verify 94.2kg total weight is within buoyancy margin of 1200mm x 600mm HDPE float
- [ ] **Solar panel sizing:** Confirm panel dimensions fit 461.2 x 461.2mm top platform
- [ ] **Battery ventilation:** LiFePO4 is safe but verify no off-gassing path needed per manufacturer spec

---

## 11. Success Criteria

- [ ] All components physically fit within designated zones and compartments
- [ ] All hull penetrations pressure-tested to 1 bar (IP68 verification)
- [ ] Weight distribution maintains stable righting moment (CG below waterline)
- [ ] Total weight within float buoyancy margin
- [ ] Full system powers on and all sensors report via serial bridge
- [ ] LoRa link established from both modules
- [ ] UV lamp cycles via relay command
- [ ] Health circuit detects Main circuit failure (watchdog test)
- [ ] Door panel provides field access to Zone B without tools (or with single tool)
- [ ] No EMI interference between inverter and LoRa (signal quality test)

---

## 12. Amendments

This spec will be amended as pending audits are completed and as component-level specs (SPEC-005 through SPEC-011) are finalized. All amendments logged to ADS with `spec_ref: SPEC-012`.

---

*"Integration is where theory meets metal. Every millimetre matters."*

## 3. Zone Layout

The buoy is divided into four physical zones:


### 3.1 Zone A: Tower Top (Exposed)

| **Upper shelf** | (outside box or in box) | LoRa Module (Main) + LoRa Module (Health) | Near top for shortest SMA antenna cable runs to Zone A. |
| **Inside either box** | With desiccant | HC-SR501 PIR | Must be sealed with desiccant (HARDWARE_AUDIT: humidity-fatal). |

> **PENDING:** Exact internal dimensions of each watertight box must be measured from the physical unit to confirm component fit. Assignment above is based on component dimensions vs. relative box sizes.

Detection window points toward door panel (which has ventilation gaps). If detection through the box wall is insufficient, mount inside a dedicated IP67 mini-enclosure with IR-transparent window.

### 3.3 Zone C: Float Hull (Sealed)

**Environment:** Sealed HDPE hull, potential condensation, no direct water ingress.
**Access:** Through tower base opening only (pre-assembly or major maintenance).
**IP Rating:** Hull itself provides IP68 when sealed.

| Component | Weight | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| LiFePO4 50Ah battery | ~6.0kg | Centre-bottom for ballast stability |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~1.5kg | Opposite side from battery for weight distribution |
| 30A Relay board | ~0.2kg | Near inverter, flyback diode REQUIRED (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| Cable bus (to Zone B) | -- | Through tower base grommet |
| Cable bus (to Zone D) | -- | Through hull bottom penetrations |

**INVERTER EMI WARNING (HARDWARE_AUDIT):** Modified sine wave inverter produces high EMI. Mount minimum 300mm from LoRa antennas. The float hull provides natural shielding from Zone B electronics. Ensure thermal venting pathway exists -- passive convection through tower base opening.

### 3.4 Zone D: Underwater (Submerged)

**Environment:** Full saltwater immersion (Sagres, Portugal -- high salinity Atlantic).
**Access:** Haul out only.
**IP Rating:** IP68 mandatory for all components and connectors.
**Material:** SS316 only (HARDWARE_AUDIT: SS304 will tea-stain in Sagres salinity).

| Component | Mount Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| DS18B20 (temperature) | Keel bracket, 130mm depth | MUST use stainless steel probe housing (HARDWARE_AUDIT: TO-92 not water-safe) |
| pH Sensor | Keel bracket | Dev only, expect drift >24h (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| TDS Sensor | Keel bracket | Requires baseline calibration (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| JSN-SR04T transducer | Hull bottom, external | IP67 rated transducer, control board in Zone C (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | Keel bracket, angled down | 230V AC via inverter, Weipu SP21 connector |

---

## 4. Hull Penetrations

All hull penetrations use Weipu SP-series IP68 connectors (HARDWARE_AUDIT). Standardize pinouts to prevent cross-wiring.

### 4.1 Penetration Map

| ID | Location | Connector | Purpose | Pin Count | Cable |
|----|----------|-----------|---------|-----------|-------|
| P1 | Float top → Tower base | Grommet (sealed) | Main cable bus (Zone C ↔ Zone B) | Multi-conductor | Tinned copper, shielded |
| P2 | Float bottom (port) | Weipu SP21 | UV lamp power (230V AC) | 3 (L/N/E) | Marine-grade, tinned |
| P3 | Float bottom (starboard) | Weipu SP13 | Sensor bundle (DS18B20, pH, TDS) | 6 | Tinned copper, shielded |
| P4 | Float bottom (fore) | Weipu SP13 | JSN-SR04T ultrasonic | 4 (VCC/Trig/Echo/GND) | Tinned copper |
| P5 | Tower top (platform edge) | SMA bulkhead x2 | LoRa antenna feedthrough | Coax | RG-174 or RG-316 |
| P6 | Tower side (upper) | Weipu SP13 | Camera cable (IMX378) | 4 | Shielded, short run |

### 4.2 Pinout Standard

**SP21 (Power/UV) -- Penetration P2:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | Live (230V AC) | Brown |
| 2 | Neutral | Blue |
| 3 | Earth | Green/Yellow |

**SP13 (Sensor Bundle) -- Penetration P3:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | DS18B20 Data (OneWire) | Yellow |
| 2 | DS18B20 VCC (3.3V) | Red |
| 3 | pH Analog Signal | White |
| 4 | TDS Analog Signal | Orange |
| 5 | Common VCC (5V) | Red/White |
| 6 | Common GND | Black |

**SP13 (Ultrasonic) -- Penetration P4:**
| Pin | Function | Wire Colour |
|-----|----------|-------------|
| 1 | VCC (5V) | Red |
| 2 | Trigger | Yellow |
| 3 | Echo | Green |
| 4 | GND | Black |

---

## 5. Cable Routing

### 5.1 Zone D → Zone C (through hull bottom penetrations P2, P3, P4)

```
Sensors (keel) ──[P3]──→ Float hull interior ──→ JSN-SR04T control board
UV lamp (keel)  ──[P2]──→ Float hull interior ──→ Relay → Inverter
Ultrasonic      ──[P4]──→ Float hull interior ──→ JSN-SR04T control board
```

### 5.2 Zone C → Zone B (through tower base grommet P1)

```
Battery ──→ Victron MPPT ──[P1]──→ RPi 5 power, RPi 3 power
Sensor signals ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) analog/digital inputs
Relay control ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) digital output
JSN-SR04T signals ──[P1]──→ Arduino Mega (Main) digital I/O
```

### 5.3 Zone B → Zone A (through antenna feedthrough P5, camera cable P6)

```
LoRa (Main) ──[SMA]──→ Antenna (tower leg)
LoRa (Health) ──[SMA]──→ Antenna (tower leg, opposite)
Camera ──[P6]──→ IMX378 (tower leg, mid-height)
```

### 5.4 Cable Management Rules

1. **All cables tinned copper** (HARDWARE_AUDIT: standard copper wicks salt air)
2. **Signal cables shielded** where running parallel to power cables (>100mm separation where possible)
3. **Cable ties: SS316** (nylon degrades in UV within months)
4. **Drip loops** on all cables entering sealed zones (prevent water wicking)
5. **Strain relief** at all connector interfaces
6. **Label all cables** at both ends with penetration ID (P1-P6)

---

## 6. Weight Budget

### 6.1 Component Weights

| Component | Weight (g) | Zone |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| RPi 5 4GB + case + cooler | ~120 | B |
| RPi 3 B+ + case | ~80 | B |
| Arduino Mega x2 | ~74 (37 each) | B |
| Victron MPPT 75/15 | ~200 | B |
| LoRa modules x2 | ~40 | B |
| 3.5" Touchscreen | ~80 | B |
| HC-SR501 PIR | ~15 | B |
| LiFePO4 50Ah battery | ~6000 | C |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~1500 | C |
| 30A Relay board | ~200 | C |
| JSN-SR04T control board | ~50 | C |
| Cables, connectors, misc | ~800 | All |
| DS18B20 probe (SS housing) | ~100 | D |
| pH sensor + probe | ~150 | D |
| TDS sensor + probe | ~100 | D |
| JSN-SR04T transducer | ~80 | D |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | ~500 | D |
| IMX378 camera + housing | ~100 | A |
| Solar panel | ~2000 | A |
| **TOTAL ELECTRONICS** | **~12,189** | -- |

### 6.2 Weight Distribution

| Zone | Total Weight | % of Electronics |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| A (Tower Top) | ~2,100g | 17% |
| B (Tower Interior) | ~609g | 5% |
| C (Float Hull) | ~8,550g | 70% |
| D (Underwater) | ~930g | 8% |

**Centre of gravity analysis:** 70% of electronics weight is in the float hull (Zone C), with the 6kg battery at centre-bottom. This is favourable for stability -- low centre of gravity. The 2kg solar panel on top is the main top-heavy element; the wide 1200mm float base provides adequate righting moment.

### 6.3 Total Buoy Weight

| Item | Weight |
|------|--------|
| Steel frame (Model 2600Fx) | 80.0 kg |
| Electronics (all zones) | ~12.2 kg |
| Fasteners, brackets, sealant | ~2.0 kg (est.) |
| **Total deployed weight** | **~94.2 kg** |

---

## 7. Thermal Management

### 7.1 Heat Sources

| Component | Max Power Dissipation | Zone | Mitigation |
|-----------|-----------------------|------|------------|
| RPi 5 | ~6W (under load) | B | Active cooler (procured), ventilation |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~15W (at load) | C | Passive convection through tower base |
| Victron MPPT 75/15 | ~2W | B | Passive, low concern |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | Heat at lamp (Zone D) | D | Water-cooled by ocean |

### 7.2 Summer Thermal Risk (Sagres, Portugal)

Ambient air temperature can reach 40°C in summer. Inside the sealed float hull, temperatures could reach 55-60°C without ventilation.

**Mitigations:**
1. **Float hull:** Passive convection path through tower base grommet (P1). Do NOT hermetically seal tower base.
2. **Tower interior:** Door panel gaps provide natural ventilation. Waterproof box has IP67 vents with desiccant.
3. **IMX378 camera:** Verify thermal limits for continuous summer operation (HARDWARE_AUDIT pending audit).
4. **White/light colour on float exterior** to reduce solar heat absorption.

---

## 8. Environmental Protection

### 8.1 Material Standards (HARDWARE_AUDIT)

| Category | Standard | Rationale |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| External fasteners | SS316 | SS304 tea-stains in Sagres salinity |
| External brackets | SS316 or POM-C | Corrosion resistance |
| Wiring | Tinned copper | Prevents internal salt-air corrosion wicking |
| Connectors | Weipu SP21/SP13 (IP68) | Proven marine-grade |
| Hull penetration sealant | Marine-grade polyurethane | UV-resistant, flexible |
| Enclosure | IP68 (hull), IP67 (tower box) | Full submersion rated |

### 8.2 Corrosion Prevention

1. All dissimilar metal junctions insulated with nylon washers (prevent galvanic corrosion)
2. Sacrificial zinc anode on keel bracket (protect SS316 fasteners)
3. Anti-seize compound on all threaded SS316 connections (prevent galling)
4. Annual inspection cycle for connector O-ring integrity

---

## 9. Assembly Sequence

**Pre-condition:** All components tested on bench per SPEC-005, SPEC-006, SPEC-007, SPEC-008.

| Step | Action | Zone | Checkpoint |
|------|--------|------|------------|
| 1 | Install hull penetration connectors P2, P3, P4 | C/D boundary | Pressure test each penetration to 1 bar |
| 2 | Route sensor cables through P3, P4; attach to keel bracket | D | Continuity test all sensor lines |
| 3 | Mount UV lamp on keel bracket; route power through P2 | D | Insulation resistance test (230V cable) |
| 4 | Mount DS18B20, pH, TDS probes on keel bracket | D | Verify probe housing seal integrity |
| 5 | Install JSN-SR04T transducer on hull bottom; control board inside hull | C/D | Ultrasonic echo test in air |
| 6 | Install LiFePO4 battery at hull centre-bottom | C | Secure with SS316 strap, verify polarity |
| 7 | Install inverter + relay board in hull | C | Verify relay flyback diode present |
| 8 | Route main cable bus through tower base grommet P1 | C→B | Label all conductors, drip loop |
| 9 | Mount Victron MPPT on lower shelf (or inside lower box if space allows) | B | Connect battery + solar input |
| 10 | Install RPi 5 + Arduino Mega (Main) into lower watertight box; seal | B | Serial connection test |
| 11 | Install RPi 3 B+ + Arduino Mega (Health) into upper watertight box; seal | B | Serial connection test |
| 12 | Mount LoRa modules on upper shelf; route SMA to P5 | B→A | RF link test (pair) |
| 13 | Mount PIR sensor inside waterproof box with desiccant | B | Motion detection test |
| 14 | Mount IMX378 camera on tower leg; route cable through P6 | A→B | Image capture test |
| 15 | Mount solar panel on top platform; connect to MPPT | A→B | Charge voltage test |
| 16 | Install door panel; seal edges | B | Visual inspection |
| 17 | **Full system integration test** | All | All sensors reporting, relay cycling, LoRa link, health heartbeat |

---

## 10. Pending Audits (from HARDWARE_AUDIT.md)

These must be completed before final integration:

- [ ] **Everbeam 50W UV:** Confirm 230V AC current draw for inverter sizing
- [ ] **IMX378 Camera:** Verify thermal limits for summer port operation (Sagres ~40°C ambient)
- [ ] **NE555 Circuit:** Audit relay spark suppression (flyback diode) for 30A DC load
- [ ] **Float buoyancy:** Verify 94.2kg total weight is within buoyancy margin of 1200mm x 600mm HDPE float
- [ ] **Solar panel sizing:** Confirm panel dimensions fit 461.2 x 461.2mm top platform
- [ ] **Battery ventilation:** LiFePO4 is safe but verify no off-gassing path needed per manufacturer spec

---

## 11. Success Criteria

- [ ] All components physically fit within designated zones and compartments
- [ ] All hull penetrations pressure-tested to 1 bar (IP68 verification)
- [ ] Weight distribution maintains stable righting moment (CG below waterline)
- [ ] Total weight within float buoyancy margin
- [ ] Full system powers on and all sensors report via serial bridge
- [ ] LoRa link established from both modules
- [ ] UV lamp cycles via relay command
- [ ] Health circuit detects Main circuit failure (watchdog test)
- [ ] Door panel provides field access to Zone B without tools (or with single tool)
- [ ] No EMI interference between inverter and LoRa (signal quality test)

---

## 12. Amendments

This spec will be amended as pending audits are completed and as component-level specs (SPEC-005 through SPEC-011) are finalized. All amendments logged to ADS with `spec_ref: SPEC-012`.

---

*"Integration is where theory meets metal. Every millimetre matters."*
specs_content.SPEC-012_INTEGRATION:
# SPEC-012: Physical Integration & Enclosure

**Status:** DRAFT
**Priority:** HIGH
**Owner:** Integration_Engineer (build), Systems_Architect (spec)
**Created:** 2026-02-05
**References:** SPEC-000 (Target Architecture), SPEC-005 (Dev Sensor Platform), SPEC-006 (Health Circuit), SPEC-009 (Oil Detection), SPEC-010 (Power Autonomy)
**Source Documents:** Final Drawings of customed buoy.pdf, Door Panel Technical Requirements (DEXON Marine), Mauser.pt invoices 2025EC1351975 & 2026EC1375416, HARDWARE_AUDIT.md

---

## 1. Purpose

This specification defines the physical integration of all OceanPulse electronic subsystems into the Model 2600Fx buoy frame. It covers zone layout, shelf assignments, cable routing, hull penetrations, weight distribution, thermal management, and assembly sequence -- all with millimetric precision derived from the manufacturer's technical drawings.

---

## 2. Buoy Frame Dimensions (Model 2600Fx)

Source: `Final Drawings of customed buoy.pdf`

### 2.1 Overall Dimensions

| Measurement | Value | Notes |
|-------------|-------|-------|
| **Float diameter** | 1200mm | Circular HDPE float body |
| **Float height** | 600mm | Vertical height of float body |
| **Keel depth** | 130mm | Below float bottom |
| **Tower frame height** | 782mm | Structural frame from float top |
| **Total above float** | 1025mm | Tower + top platform + light mount |
| **Total structure height** | 1755mm | Keel bottom to top (130 + 600 + 1025) |
| **Frame weight** | 80kg | Steel frame only, excluding electronics |

### 2.2 Tower Structure

| Measurement | Value | Source |
|-------------|-------|--------|
| **Top platform** | 461.2 x 461.2mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Internal tower height** | 974mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening height** | 824.5mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening width (bottom)** | 419.8mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements |
| **Door opening width (top)** | 333.6mm | Door Panel Tech Requirements (trapezoidal) |
| **Door material** | Carbon steel Q235 | Current frame: raw steel (dev unit). HDG-identical frame arriving in ~20-25 days (ETA late Feb 2026). Final integration uses HDG frame only. |

### 2.3 Tower Interior Layout

The tower interior contains **2 shelves** with **2 watertight boxes** (one per shelf).

| Element | Position | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| **Lower shelf** | Lower tower section | Holds the larger watertight box |
| **Upper shelf** | Upper tower section | Holds the smaller watertight box |
| **Lower watertight box** | On lower shelf | Slightly larger than upper box |
| **Upper watertight box** | On upper shelf | Slightly smaller than lower box |

Drawing reference dimensions (side view, 塑料防水盒 annotation):

| Measurement | Value | Interpretation |
|-------------|-------|----------------|
| Box width | 300mm | Width of watertight boxes |
| Interval 1 | 170mm | Lower box or spacing |
| Interval 2 | 197mm | Shelf gap or larger box height |
| Interval 3 | 170mm | Upper box or spacing |

> **NOTE:** Exact internal dimensions of each watertight box to be measured from physical unit. The drawing intervals (170/197/170mm) require physical verification against the 2-shelf layout.

---

## 3. Zone Layout

The buoy is divided into four physical zones:

```
        ┌─── Zone A: Tower Top (exposed) ───┐
        │  Solar panel mount, LoRa antennas  │
        │  Nav light position, camera        │
        ├─── Zone B: Tower Interior ─────────┤  ← Door panel access
        │  All primary electronics           │
        │  Waterproof box + shelf system     │
        │  974mm internal height             │
        ├─── Zone C: Float Hull ─────────────┤  ← Sealed, no field access
        │  Battery, inverter, ballast        │
        │  Access via tower base only        │
        ├─── Zone D: Underwater ─────────────┤
        │  Sensors, UV lamp, keel            │
        │  130mm below float                 │
        └────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3.1 Zone A: Tower Top (Exposed)

**Environment:** Fully exposed to weather, salt spray, UV radiation, bird strike.
**Access:** Open (no enclosure).
**IP Rating Required:** All components IP67 minimum.

| Component | Mount Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Solar panel | Top platform (461.2 x 461.2mm) | Angled bracket, SS316 bolts |
| LoRa antenna (Main) | Tower leg, upper | SMA cable to Zone B |
| LoRa antenna (Health) | Tower leg, opposite | SMA cable to Zone B |
| IMX378 Camera | Tower leg, mid-height | IP68 housing required, facing outward |
| HC-SR501 PIR sensor | **NOT HERE** -- humidity fatal | See Zone B |

### 3.2 Zone B: Tower Interior (Protected)

**Environment:** Sheltered by tower frame + door panel, not hermetically sealed.
**Access:** Door panel (trapezoidal, 419.8mm bottom x 333.6mm top x 824.5mm height).
**Protection:** Two watertight boxes on two shelves, desiccant packs inside each box.

**CRITICAL CONSTRAINT:** All components inserted through door opening must fit within 333.6mm (minimum width at top). Diagonal insertion possible for items up to ~540mm.

**Layout: 2 shelves, 2 watertight boxes**

| Location | Box | Component Assignments | Notes |
|----------|-----|----------------------|-------|
| **Lower shelf** | Larger watertight box | RPi 5 (Main) + Arduino Mega (Main) | Main circuit. Larger box accommodates RPi 5 (85x56mm) + Mega (101x53mm) + wiring. Victron MPPT 75/15 (100x113x40mm) also fits here if box allows. |
| **Lower shelf** | (outside box) | Victron MPPT 75/15 | If MPPT doesn't fit inside box, mount on shelf beside it. MPPT is not humidity-critical. |
| **Upper shelf** | Smaller watertight box | RPi 3 B+ (Health) + Arduino Mega (Health) | Health circuit. RPi 3 (85x56mm) + Mega (101x53mm). Smaller box sufficient. |
| **Upper shelf** | (outside box or in box) | LoRa Module (Main) + LoRa Module (Health) | Near top for shortest SMA antenna cable runs to Zone A. |
| **Inside either box** | With desiccant | HC-SR501 PIR | Must be sealed with desiccant (HARDWARE_AUDIT: humidity-fatal). |
| **Tower top area** | -- | Antenna SMA feedthroughs | Route through tower structure to Zone A. |
| **Tower base area** | -- | Cable management | P1 grommet entry point, drip loops, cable routing. |

> **PENDING:** Exact internal dimensions of each watertight box must be measured from the physical unit to confirm component fit. Assignment above is based on component dimensions vs. relative box sizes.

**PIR Humidity Mitigation (HARDWARE_AUDIT):** The HC-SR501 is extremely humidity-sensitive. It MUST be mounted inside one of the watertight boxes with desiccant packs. Detection window points toward door panel (which has ventilation gaps). If detection through the box wall is insufficient, mount inside a dedicated IP67 mini-enclosure with IR-transparent window.

### 3.3 Zone C: Float Hull (Sealed)

**Environment:** Sealed HDPE hull, potential condensation, no direct water ingress.
**Access:** Through tower base opening only (pre-assembly or major maintenance).
**IP Rating:** Hull itself provides IP68 when sealed.

| Component | Weight | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| LiFePO4 50Ah battery | ~6.0kg | Centre-bottom for ballast stability |
| Green Cell Inverter | ~1.5kg | Opposite side from battery for weight distribution |
| 30A Relay board | ~0.2kg | Near inverter, flyback diode REQUIRED (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| Cable bus (to Zone B) | -- | Through tower base grommet |
| Cable bus (to Zone D) | -- | Through hull bottom penetrations |

**INVERTER EMI WARNING (HARDWARE_AUDIT):** Modified sine wave inverter produces high EMI. Mount minimum 300mm from LoRa antennas. The float hull provides natural shielding from Zone B electronics. Ensure thermal venting pathway exists -- passive convection through tower base opening.

### 3.4 Zone D: Underwater (Submerged)

**Environment:** Full saltwater immersion (Sagres, Portugal -- high salinity Atlantic).
**Access:** Haul out only.
**IP Rating:** IP68 mandatory for all components and connectors.
**Material:** SS316 only (HARDWARE_AUDIT: SS304 will tea-stain in Sagres salinity).

| Component | Mount Location | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| DS18B20 (temperature) | Keel bracket, 130mm depth | MUST use stainless steel probe housing (HARDWARE_AUDIT: TO-92 not water-safe) |
| pH Sensor | Keel bracket | Dev only, expect drift >24h (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| TDS Sensor | Keel bracket | Requires baseline calibration (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| JSN-SR04T transducer | Hull bottom, external | IP67 rated transducer, control board in Zone C (HARDWARE_AUDIT) |
| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | Keel bracket, angled down | 230V AC via inverter, Weipu SP21 connector |

---

## 4. Hull Penetrations

All hull penetrations use Weipu SP-series IP68 connectors (HARDWARE_AUDIT). Standardize pinouts to prevent cross-wiring.

### 4.1 Penetration Map

| ID | Location | Connector | Purpose | Pin Count | Cable |

| P1 | Float top → Tower base | Grommet (sealed) | Main cable bus (Zone C ↔ Zone B) | Multi-conductor | Tinned copper, shielded |

| P3 | Float bottom (starboard) | Weipu SP13 | Sensor bundle (DS18B20, pH, TDS) | 6 | Tinned copper, shielded |
| P4 | Float bottom (fore) | Weipu SP13 | JSN-SR04T ultrasonic | 4 (VCC/Trig/Echo/GND) | Tinned copper |
| P5 | Tower top (platform edge) | SMA bulkhead x2 | LoRa antenna feedthrough | Coax | RG-174 or RG-316 |

## 5. Cable Routing

### 5.4 Cable Management Rules

1. **All cables tinned copper** (HARDWARE_AUDIT: standard copper wicks salt air)
2. **Signal cables shielded** where running parallel to power cables (>100mm separation where possible)

4. **Drip loops** on all cables entering sealed zones (prevent water wicking)

| UV Lamp (Everbeam 50W) | ~500 | D |
| IMX378 camera + housing | ~100 | A |

### 6.2 Weight Distribution

| Zone | Total Weight | % of Electronics |

| A (Tower Top) | ~2,100g | 17% |
| B (Tower Interior) | ~609g | 5% |
| C (Float Hull) | ~8,550g | 70% |

**Centre of gravity analysis:** 70% of electronics weight is in the float hull (Zone C), with the 6kg battery at centre-bottom. This is favourable for stability -- low centre of gravity. The 2kg solar panel on top is the main top-heavy element; the wide 1200mm float base provides adequate righting moment.

### 6.3 Total Buoy Weight

| Fasteners, brackets, sealant | ~2.0 kg (est.) |
